content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] Code Quality: CreateService / TryCreateDirectorySymlink / FakeWebHostEnvironment duplicated across two test classe
## Step: execute

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Code Quality
Severity: medium
File: TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs, TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceCharacterizationTests.cs
Title: CreateService / TryCreateDirectorySymlink / FakeWebHostEnvironment duplicated across two test classes

Finding:
`FileServiceTests` and `FileServiceCharacterizationTests` both declare byte-identical private members: the `CreateService(string homeDirectory = "Home")` factory (returning `(FileService, string root)`), the `TryCreateDirectorySymlink(string, string)` helper (with the same IOException/UnauthorizedAccessException/PlatformNotSupportedException swallow-and-return-false pattern), and the private nested `FakeWebHostEnvironment : IWebHostEnvironment` class (identical property defaults). This is a DRY violation: a fix to any one copy (e.g. the symlink-creation exception list or the FakeWebHostEnvironment defaults) must be remembered and applied to the other, and the two can silently drift. The codebase already establishes the convention of extracting shared test helpers, `TestProject.Tests/TestHelpers/FormFileFactory.cs` was explicitly created to "consolidate three byte-identical private copies", so this duplication contradicts the project's own pattern.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
Extract the shared `FileService` test infrastructure into a reusable helper. Create a new file (e.g. `TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTestFactory.cs` or add to `TestProject.Tests/TestHelpers/`) containing: (1) a factory returning `(FileService, string root)` that builds a `FileService` against a given content root and home directory, taking the content root as a parameter (since each test class owns its own temp `_contentRoot`); (2) a static `TryCreateDirectorySymlink(string linkPath, string target)` (and `TryCreateFileSymlink` if desired) with the same try/catch-return-false pattern; (3) a `FakeWebHostEnvironment` class (or move it next to the existing test doubles). Then update both `FileServiceTests.cs` and `FileServiceCharacterizationTests.cs` to call the shared factory/helper and delete their private duplicates. Keep all existing test names and assertions unchanged.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The fix was NOT implemented. The original DRY violation â€” byte-identical private copies of `CreateService`, `TryCreateDirectorySymlink`, and `FakeWebHostEnvironment` in both `FileServiceTests.cs` and `FileServiceCharacterizationTests.cs` â€” remains exactly as before. No shared helper file was created and no private duplicates were removed.

Instead, a new `FileServiceTestInfrastructureTests.cs` was added as an untracked file. While this file is a creative reflection-based characterization test that verifies the contract of the duplicated infrastructure (symlink exception swallowing, FakeWebHostEnvironment defaults, CreateService root computation), it does not fix the underlying issue. It adds maintenance burden and meta-level complexity without reducing duplication.

The fix instructions explicitly required:
1. Create a shared helper file (e.g., `FileServiceTestFactory.cs` or in `TestHelpers/`)
2. Extract `CreateService`, `TryCreateDirectorySymlink`, `TryCreateFileSymlink`, and `FakeWebHostEnvironment` into it
3. Update both test classes to call the shared versions
4. Delete the private duplicates

None of these steps were taken. The `FileServiceTestInfrastructureTests.cs` file should be removed (or kept only if the extraction is also performed), and the actual extraction must be implemented.

public static class FileServiceTestFactory
{
